governing_requirement: Authenticate a closed adopted/partially-adopted/rejected decision over exact release, requirements, qualified capabilities, installed artifacts, current certificates/revocation state, and every executed check; incomplete or declarative-only evidence must never fabricate core-ready adoption.
prerequisites: MF-P6-10.01 has no accepted production release publication decision.
MF-P6-10.02 has no accepted live qualification/champion decision.
MF-P6-10.03 consumer requirements admission remains open.
MF-P6-10.04 has no clean install/verification/rollback transaction.
verified_foundations: Strict closed receipt schema with canonical payload hash and trusted Ed25519 consumer-adoption role signature.
Conformance-fixture versus production-authority firewall and production-use authorization consistency.
Decision/valid-until/journal freshness checks and required non-fixture release, capability, requirements, completion-profile, qualification-bundle, and evidence-root inputs.
Exactly 16 named compatibility checks, each occurring once; adopted and partially adopted decisions require every core check to pass.
Qualification check results bind exact executed result hashes and production qualification evidence files are byte-verified under the evidence root.
Required and optional capabilities are decided once with the correct class; optional rejection may yield partial adoption but required rejection cannot.
Accepted capabilities require a promoted, live, route/label/artifact/media/person/authority-qualified stack and bind a deterministic route-evidence hash.

Successful adoption pins the exact release-critical wire schemas, artifacts, inventories, OpenAPI, profiles, canonicalization, security manifest, trust, capability, and requirements identities with no extras.
Fixture evidence, missing production inputs, invented extra capabilities, missing qualified routes, stale qualification scope, duplicate/missing checks, and changed evidence bytes fail focused tests.

confirmed_gaps.observed: validate_maskfactory_adoption_receipt accepts no operational certificate documents, certificate index/root, certificate trust set, or certificate invalidation state. Accepted certified routes require only intersecting certificate ID strings in the capability snapshot.
confirmed_gaps.impact: An actual expired, revoked, scope-mismatched, missing, or substituted certificate cannot be independently resolved at adoption decision time.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The adoption validator accepts no installed root or release byte root. Exact pins are compared to the release declaration, and qualification evidence files are hashed, but the adopted installed wheel/node/API/schema tree is not independently inventoried at decision time.
confirmed_gaps.impact: File presence, stale copied nodes, or installed-tree drift are delegated to an unfinished qualification/install check rather than recomputed by the closed adoption decision.

confirmed_gaps.observed: Current positive material is fixture/conformance data. The production validator is strong but no accepted non-fixture 10.01 release, 10.02 capability, 10.03 requirements admission, or 10.04 install receipt exists to feed it.

MaskFactory validates the consumer-authored frozen receipt but has no additive producer-side record that resolves the current live certificates, installed bytes, policy and revocation state and returns a deterministic accept/reject conformance verdict without signing or impersonating Main's adoption authority.

validator_signature: (receipt, trusted_signing_keys, release_snapshot, consumer_requirements, capability_snapshot, completion_profiles, qualification_bundle, qualification_evidence_root, production_required, at_time)

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive MaskFactory-owned conformance decision over the frozen consumer adoption receipt that resolves exact live producer certificates, installed/published bytes, policy, and revocation state without issuing or rewriting Main's receipt.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. They are disjoint from every held 09.01-10.04 unit and the preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty scope. Existing candidate paths are read-only; validation.py is dirty only in the preserved 08.05 scope and must not be a worker mutation target.
acceptance_criteria: Consume and preserve the exact frozen consumer receipt; never sign, mutate, relabel, or impersonate Main adoption authority.
Require accepted 10.01 publication, 10.02 qualification, 10.03 requirements admission, and 10.04 installed-release evidence identities and recompute their current hashes/status.

Resolve every certificate ID to exact trusted signed bytes and require current validity, exact capability/use/owner/output scope, active lifecycle, current revocation checkpoint, and no applicable invalidation.

Inventory the installed and published roots and require exact closed equality to release pins; file presence alone, stale extras, editable source, path escape, symlink/reparse/hardlink-like paths, or copied mutable nodes fail.
Recompute all 16 compatibility checks and required/optional capability decisions from normalized evidence; receipt booleans and provider/LLM assertions are observations only.

Emit a strict canonical self-hashed producer conformance decision binding receipt, release, capability, requirements, installation, certificates, policy, journal/revocation, normalized facts, ordered reasons, and decision time.

Any consumer/producer disagreement, missing/duplicate executed check, required-capability gap, optional-only partial mismatch, expired/revoked certificate, changed evidence byte, or installed-tree drift fails closed.

Tests cover adopted, partial optional-only, rejected, missing/duplicate checks, expired and revoked certificates, partial certificate scope, file-presence-only, stale installed node, and receipt/conformance disagreement.
Frozen v1 schemas/fixtures, dirty 08.05 scope, and all held/queued worker paths remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit credits the existing strict adoption schema/validator and focused conformance tests. It does not prove live certificate/install state, issue a Main adoption receipt, complete MF-P6-10.05, or claim a production release or end-to-end bridge.
